TextENGNew: "I don't understand...@k@vS10/62/410101514. Did that Kurosawa guy betray you or something?@k@vS10/62/410101515. Cheating, lies... is that what you couldn't forgive?"
"No... Takumi-kun would never do that.@k@vS10/42/410600423_1. I just... wanted to erase him. That's all it was."
changeReason: Original 'get rid of' vs new 'erase.' JPN 消す means erase/make disappear specifically.

TextENGNew: "Erase...?"
changeReason: Original 'Get... rid of?' vs new 'Erase...?' -- consistent with JPN 消して.

TextENGNew: "Yes, that's all.@k@vS10/42/410600425. Because I... loved Takumi-kun so much."
"...That doesn't add up.@k@vS10/62/410101517. If you loved him... why would you kill him?"
"Takumi-kun and I have been together for almost seven years now.@k@vS10/42/410600428. We met... right after we started university..."
Towada-san raised her head, her eyes taking on a faraway look.
Behind her eyelids, she was seeing a slightly weathered campus building, set a short distance from the city center...
changeReason: Original 'image emerged on the back of her eyelids' vs new 'Behind her eyelids, she was seeing' -- also 'old school building' vs 'weathered campus building.'

TextENGNew: A four-story school building visible beyond a stretch of green lawn.
changeReason: Old misreads spatial relationship: says 'from the fourth floor' but Japanese describes a 4-story building beyond a lawn.

TextENGNew: Our class was in the very last classroom on the far end of the building.
I finally got accepted into the college I had been hoping to attend, so everything was looking bright.

...But things were a bit difficult for me because I was a year younger than my classmates... I started to develop an inferiority complex about it... and after enrolling, I spent most of my time alone.
"Hey, Towada-san.@k@vS10/00/410700040. Are you interested in attending a live concert next Sunday?"
One day,
a male classmate spoke to me for the very first time.
His smooth hair was a bit disheveled.
His clothes were a bit sloppy, like he didn't try hard to dress well... but even though I normally would have found that detail unappealing, it actually gave me a good feeling...
He was still quite young and had a youthful look on his face.
The thing about him I found most attractive was his eyes. ...Takumi-kun's eyes sparkled. They seemed to radiate all the enjoyment filling his heart.
"A concert...?"
"Yeah, a concert.@k@vS10/00/410700042. Do you like music, Towada-san?"
"Y-yeah... a little.@k@vS10/42/410600432. Who is performing at this concert?"
"I am. Me.@k@vS10/00/410700044. Who else would it be?"
"I'm... putting together a band.@k@vS10/00/410700046. Actually, I write my own songs too... and I'm rather proud of my work."
...Honestly, I was totally shocked.

I didn't have any special skills of my own, so for someone to be able to pick up music while also studying for school, it felt like he was from a totally different world... Not just a celebrity, but a divine being.
He even did his own composition and spoke to people to convince them to attend his concerts. That's not something you can do without a great deal of confidence in your skills.
And a person with such unprecedented talent was standing directly in front of me, right now...
So, I was completely star-struck... It was a wonderful feeling.
"So? ...How about it?@k@vS10/00/410700048. If you're busy, I understand..."
I was so stunned by the sudden situation that I couldn't find the words. Perhaps due to that, his voice suddenly grew uneasy.
...After seeing that, I understood.
Of course. This guy was putting on a brave face for my sake.
All just for me, the one who stuck out in our class...
"...Is that really ok?@k@vS10/42/410600436. I don't know much about music though..."
"O-of course! That's fine!@k@vS10/00/410700050. It'll be great, I promise!"
As soon as I responded that it was ok, he put on a huge, bright smile.
Honestly, he was like a child...
But I found that innocence wonderful, from the bottom of my heart.
"So... where are you performing?@k@vS10/42/410600438. Can you tell me the location?"
"R-right! Here's a ticket and the flyer!@k@vS10/00/410700052. ...Oh, don't worry about money! It's completely free!@k@vS10/00/410700053. Alright, it's a promise then! I'll be waiting for you!!"
Takumi-kun wrapped his little finger around my little finger as he promised that with a beaming smile on his face, then he ran out of the classroom.
The warmth of his touch still lingered in my little finger.
That was... maybe the first time in my entire life locking fingers with a boy.
My heart... was beating fast.
And it didn't take much time after that before we got together.
Since we were in the same class, we were always together. The two of us went to all sorts of places together every week, and I went to see all his concerts.
He... Takumi Kurosawa-kun was playing the guitar, and was fortunate enough to have a bunch of fans.
Fan girls would come to the concerts and stare longingly at Takumi-kun. There were so many girls for Takumi-kun to choose from, and they were always cheering for him.
But even so... Takumi-kun's eyes were always on me.
I was the one he cherished most.
And that made me so happy... It was a blessing.
"I'm seriously aiming to go for the major leagues after college.."
"Major leagues...?"
"Yeah, I want to go pro.@k@vS10/00/410700056. Maybe you'll make fun of me for being reckless, but...@k@vS10/00/410700057. I really want to make a living off my music."
Indeed... I did think it a bit reckless.
Takumi-kun's music was quite wonderful, and he certainly was talented.

...But, going pro was a completely different story. Plenty of talented people put all their passion, expertise, and hard work striving for it... but only a handful of people ever make it onto the stage.
And even if that major debut did come to fruition and he was able to live off of it, there'd still be fierce competition waiting for him every single day.
Plus, this isn't the sort of world where you're rewarded simply because you put in a lot of effort. ...After all, everyone else is working hard too, naturally.
Could he really make his dreams come true in this callous, ruthless world...?
"I don't totally understand it, but... isn't it hard to make it as a professional?@k@vS10/42/410600442. ...Will it really be all right?"

"Well, that's true.@k@vS10/00/410700059. But I've got tons of lyrics I've been writing since I was a kid.@k@vS10/00/410700060. All that's left is to put music to them.@k@vS10/00/410700061. I'm rather confident in my work, you know?@k@vS10/00/410700062. Plus, you told me it was good, didn't you, Yae?"
"Y-yeah..."

"Well, I'm sure I'll have to keep working my part-time job for a little while to pay the bills.@k@vS10/00/410700064. As long as you're with me, Yae, and I can make a living just playing my music, I'll be happy.@k@vS10/00/410700065. I couldn't ask for anything else.@k@vS10/00/410700066. So, I think I'll just have to work hard."
...I enjoyed hearing Takumi-kun talk about his dreams.
Somehow, it felt different from anyone else.
He wasn't swayed by the whims of the world. Takumi-kun had his own values and he decided for himself how he wanted to live.
I was confident that he would be able to manage.
He's Takumi-kun, surely he'll be able to make his dreams come true... At least, I thought so.
Takumi-kun's eyes glittered and sparkled any time he told me about his future ambitions.
If we ever move in together, I want to get a dachshund.
I'd like to live near a big park, and try doing the radio exercises together every morning, even as we grow old.
I want to play a concert at the Budokan, and propose to you right after finishing my best song...
He was seriously considering these things, laughing like a child all the while.
But... despite that, I was feeling the opposite. I was quite anxious.
If he really does make a big hit and become a celebrity, he'll be surrounded by fans and other stars... If that were to happen, would Takumi-kun's feelings for me stay the same?
I believe that he loves me very much right now. ...But as his position changes, his perspective will change... and his values along with it.
When that happens... will I be able to follow him?
Or... will he leave me behind?
That dreadful future haunted my thoughts. Sometimes even my dreams...
And so, there came a day when I asked him about it.
"Hey, Takumi-kun...@k@vS10/42/410600445. If some horrible disaster plagued our world and forced you to choose between your music or me... which would you choose?"
"Huh? What is this...?@k@vS10/00/410700068. What is that supposed to mean?"
"Uh, umm... well.@k@vS10/42/410600447. I saw a story like that on TV the other day..."
That was a lie, of course. It truly was a ridiculous nonsensical question.
But Takumi-kun smiled like usual... and without hesitation, he gave me an answer with a serious look on his face.
"I dunno what sort of horrible disaster you're talking about, but...@k@vS10/00/410700070. If my only options are you or my music... I'd definitely choose you, Yae."
"...Really?"
"Of course.@k@vS10/00/410700072. I could live without my music, but honestly, I don't even want to imagine life without you."
"Takumi-kun..."
I was elated.
He wasn't just saying that. It felt like he truly meant it... I was deeply moved.
I always felt warm and happy when I was with Takumi-kun. Just being around him made me feel at ease...
"Takumi-kun... I love you."
"...You idiot.@k@vS10/00/410700074. I love you even more than you can imagine."
"No way! I..."
I started to speak up, but Takumi-kun kissed me.
Every single day was packed with that sweet sentiment... Those four years were absolutely delightful.
I graduated from college and got a job doing clerical work for a small trading company.
It was a company I had never heard of and it didn't have very many employees, but the company was doing solid business.
Takumi-kun, on the other hand, couldn't find a job anywhere.
Naturally, he'd always say he was chasing his dream.
Takumi-kun's life hadn't changed much since his college days.
He sincerely wanted to concentrate all his strength on his music career.
His life wasn't all that different, just that the time he spent in lectures was replaced with a part-time job. The band members' faces didn't change either.
Just like in our college days, I attended Takumi-kun's concerts every week.
So, our relationship never really settled down.
...Before long, several years had passed.
The people around us started getting married and skipping the band to take care of their kids.
Takumi-kun's band members started giving up on the band, one after another...
All my friends around the same age got their dose of reality, and started walking through life with their feet on the ground.
"Takumi-kun, are you all right...?@k@vS10/42/410600453. You've been feeling down recently, haven't you?"
"I'm all right.@k@vS10/00/410700076. ...I'm just busy at work, so I'm not getting enough sleep."
Although Takumi-kun said as much, I could tell he was feeling irritated.
He was having trouble replacing missing band members recently, and he was struggling to find the money to open a studio, or put on any concerts.
...No, maybe I was the one getting impatient.
Our love hadn't changed much since the old days... But honestly... I was starting to worry if we could really keep things the way they were.
I mean... whenever I talked to him, my anxiety about the future still nagged at my heart... smoldering like an old flame.

"Sorry... I didn't mean to make you worry.@k@vS10/00/410700078. But I'm sure things will get better soon.@k@vS10/00/410700079. No, I'll make it happen.@k@vS10/00/410700080. Actually, the producer at that record company said something about meeting up..."
Takumi-kun seemed so happy when he was talking about things back then.
...But I was only half-listening to him.
Because... I'd already heard that story many times before. He said it all the time.
...I didn't mean to make you worry.
Something about those words felt out of place.
Because, unlike in the past... something started to feel cold deep inside my heart.
Honestly... I might not have been worried that Takumi-kun was feeling down.
Maybe somewhere deep down, I was trying to use his lethargy as an excuse to tell him to set aside his dreams and live a steady lifestyle.
Our college friends were getting ready to buy condos, build houses, and starting to have children.
And... the feeling that it's not so easy to make your dreams come true was finally starting to sink in. Takumi-kun probably felt the same way...
So one day, when I came back from work...
"Hey, Takumi-kun...@k@vS10/42/410600455. Let's move in together."
I was the one to suggest it.
But the suggestion wasn't born from kindness or love.
By being just a little closer to him... I could set aside my helpless impatience, my frustrations, my anxiety about the future. I could fill all the holes in my heart.

"If we live together, our expenses will be cut in half...@k@vS10/42/410600457. And if we cook for ourselves properly, we can save a lot of money there too.@k@vS10/42/410600458. If we put some of that money toward your music career, maybe it'll make your troubles a little lighter."
"...Are you really ok with that?@k@vS10/00/410700083. Yae, you..."
"It's fine.@k@vS10/42/410600460. I want to do it."
Takumi-kun didn't answer right away. ...That seemed natural. Regardless of how I felt about it, I'd be formally agreeing to cover part of his expenses... no, perhaps almost all of them.
But... I had already decided. And I was fine with it.
"Takumi-kun, you're able to do things I could never do.@k@vS10/42/410600462. You have incredible talent, beyond my imagination.@k@vS10/42/410600463. So, I want to support you."
"There's a world out there I could never see on my own, but if I could see even a little of it standing by your side... would that be so bad?"
"No, but..."

"I'm sure it'll happen.@k@vS10/42/410600466. ...It's all right, I believe in you.@k@vS10/42/410600467. So let me make you believe too, Takumi-kun.@k@vS10/42/410600468. In the lovely world from your dreams you're having now..."
Then, Takumi-kun hugged me so tight that it hurt... and cried, just a little.
Despite the pain in my arms.
Despite the burning of those teardrops.
I felt happy... Yes, I truly did feel happy...
And the guilt of the cold, sinful emotions I had begun to embrace... felt just a little bit lighter...
And then, we started living together.
There were all sorts of problems that came from the two of us living together, and it was more difficult than I thought... but that was all blown away by the tremendous joy I got out of it.
...Really, amazingly so... Looking back on it, I was like a child.
Still, Takumi-kun was always so kind.
On paydays, he would always bring home my favorite cake.
He was happy to help out with the cooking and laundry.
When I came home tired after a tedious day at work... he'd listen to my stories and comfort me. ...Encourage me.
Just remembering those days brings a tear to my eye...
We were so full of love... Those days were irreplaceable...
Really, there was no mistaking that. It was a fact.
"I... don't want to be a bother for you, Yae...@k@vS10/00/410700088. If you're unhappy, you're allowed to tell me about it, you know?"
"Yeah..."
"Yae... you'll still be standing by my side when I make my major debut, right?"
"Well, of course...?@k@vS10/42/410600471. After all, I'm your number one fan, Takumi-kun."
...Every time I said that, a dull pain coursed through my chest.
I responded that way automatically, but another part of myself was gradually beginning to feel otherwise.
That other "me" watched Takumi-kun with cold eyes as he continued dreaming of making his major debut.
I hated that part of myself.
So... I carefully chose my words to hide it.
"...I'll support you.@k@vS10/42/410600473. I'll stand by your side... as you chase your dreams, Takumi-kun.@k@vS10/42/410600474. ...Because... I love you, Takumi-kun."

"...Your faith in me gives me the strength to keep chasing those dreams.@k@vS10/00/410700091. Your presence is what keeps me going.@k@vS10/00/410700092. ...Yae, thanks for everything.@k@vS10/00/410700093. ...I love you."
...If there's one small regret still inside me now.
It's that I wish I had believed in him more.
In him... in our love, and in myself.
Love and dreams were all we had... but I didn't need anything more to be happy.
If I could have gone on living, bathed in that warm fantasy, treasuring that precious, gentle time...
Maybe... this outcome wouldn't have transpired.
One of the lightbulbs burned out in this dimly lit assembly hall.
Towada-san briefly paused her story to gently gaze down at Kurosawa's face.
When I glanced at my watch, the time was now 3 AM.
"You seemed so happy...@k@vS10/41/410200779. So, why did it come to this...?"
"Those sweet days didn't go on for long..."
"Unfortunately, Takumi-kun changed."
Towada-san shook her head self-derisively.
One day, I came home from work to find Takumi-kun skipping work and drinking alcohol.
"What's going on...?@k@vS10/42/410600478. Don't you have work today...?"
"I took a day off."
He spoke like he was flinging the words at me.
Of course, there was no reason for him to suddenly take a day off. It's good to get a little time off, but he didn't have the vacation time to spare.
(I wonder if something is wrong...?)
I was rather tired after work that evening, but I wanted Takumi-kun to feel better... so I ran to the supermarket and prepared a feast.
But... Takumi-kun didn't say another word.
I wondered what happened... but in the end, he never told me.
Even though just a few weeks earlier, he would have grumbled out a few complaints about what was bothering him... and then would have cheered up at least a little once he got it off his chest.
"I... don't want to be a bother for you, Yae...@k@vS10/00/410700096. If you're unhappy, you're allowed to talk about it you know?"
The words he said earlier came back to me.
That... was the first time Takumi-kun ever broke a promise to me.
Takumi-kun didn't go to his part-time job the next day, or the day after.
With Takumi-kun not working, he couldn't pay the monthly rent... so I paid it in his place. After that day, I made every rent payment myself.
Takumi-kun... never said anything about it. He didn't say anything.
Before long, I started paying the water and electric bills, and even covered the cost of our meals.
Takumi-kun would wake up around noon to work on his composition and write lyrics...
That was enough to keep me convinced. I was able to encourage myself to keep working hard.
But one day... the song he wrote was so disconnected I couldn't even bring myself to listen to it...
Unfortunately, I let a few words slip out. "What is this? It's weird."
I never imagined how much that one statement could trample his motivation and self-esteem.
...As a result, he never wrote any more songs after that... He simply spent all day drinking alcohol.
I, on the other hand, spent every day struggling to keep up with my office work. It wasn't fun at all.
I smiled even when I didn't want to around superiors and coworkers, and the stress of communicating with junior colleagues piled up.
While I continued living that lifestyle... Takumi-kun was living an easygoing lifestyle more akin to retirement.
Over time... I gradually started feeling stressed around him too.
"Hey, Takumi-kun... Still not working...?"
"I'll work.@k@vS10/00/410700098. ...I'm just a little tired right now."
"...Sorry for all the trouble.@k@vS10/00/410700100. Soon, I'll definitely make a song so good it'll shock you, Yae."
"...Okay, that's good.@k@vS10/42/410600485. Looking forward to it."
I was annoyed with myself... for saying, "That's good."
I really should've said, "That's not good at all."
And I thought about sharing my honest feelings with him... but I couldn't bring myself to say it.
Takumi-kun is doing his best to make his dreams come true... so I have to support him...
I have to work harder...
I need to be more patient...
Right. As long as I can endure this...
If I love Takumi-kun... I have to endure this...
But it was gradually getting harder to maintain this lifestyle. That much was a fact.
More and more friends were getting married and having children.
Even when I spoke with them, it was difficult to maintain a conversation... I couldn't help but envy our friends for having families and happy lives.
So, I gradually fell out of touch with those friends... and started feeling really lonely.
If things stayed the way they were with Takumi-kun, having a child would be rather difficult...
It took a tremendous amount of work and my entire salary to take care of Takumi-kun, so raising a child on top of that was... absolutely impossible!
It was... heartbreaking.
But... it must be even more painful for Takumi-kun.
None of his hard work is ever recognized, even after doing so much to make his dreams come true. All that struggling... only to fail.
He must be tormented by that frustration, every single day.
So, these hardships I'm enduring are no big deal.
No big deal...
......Really, though?
Is Takumi-kun... really in that much pain?
After all... he spends all day sleeping and lazing about, doesn't he?
He's not even writing songs anymore.
His skills have gotten dull, his instruments have fallen out of tune... and now they're collecting dust in the corner of the room, aren't they?!
How is that difficult?
How is that painful?
And all the while, I've been gritting my teeth and doing my best to endure it?
Working until my body felt heavy, every single day, at a job that wasn't what I had hoped it would be, all for Takumi-kun's sake?
Does Takumi-kun... not understand how much I'm suffering?!
Does he truly comprehend how desperately I've worked to endure it?!
...It's not normal.
...It's unfair.
Why...? Why do I alone need to be patient... when Takumi-kun is off doing whatever he likes?
How much longer do I have to put up with this?!
Until tomorrow?
The next day?
Another year?!
Or maybe... forever?!
In the meantime, my birthday had arrived.
"...Haah."
My legs felt heavy.
Until now, Takumi-kun and I would always go out to eat together... but I couldn't expect that to continue with Takumi-kun not bringing in any income.
But I didn't have the heart to invite him out to eat. ...Even to celebrate my own birthday.
Still, there was also something unpleasant about needing to head home and prepare dinner on such a special day.
In the end... I had to work overtime that day, then went straight home.
I had forgotten it was my birthday...
And I'm sure Takumi-kun forgot too...
No... I was just pretending to forget...
"...I'm home."
When I came home, Takumi-kun was in his room like usual.
But... something about him looked different.
Though I expected him to be laying about... he was sitting nicely in the middle of the room... looking straight at me.
"You're late."
"Ah, yeah..."
Takumi-kun looked up with a stagnant look in his eyes.
And he looked a bit drunk, like he'd been drinking here alone.
"Sorry, I was working overtime...@k@vS10/42/410600490. Ah, I'll have dinner ready soon.@k@vS10/42/410600491. It's already a bit late today, so I'll have to manage with whatever's available..."
"What is it?"
"...Don't lie to me."
The next moment, Takumi-kun rose to his feet... and harshly grabbed me by the shoulders.
"Oww?@k@vS10/42/410600495. W-what's wrong, Takumi-kun?!"
"How can you even ask that?@k@vS10/00/410700106. ...Why are you lying to me?"
"I-I'm not lying to you...!@k@vS10/42/410600497. Takumi-kun, what are you talking about?"
"After all... today is your birthday, isn't it?"
He... remembered.
That realization made me a bit happy.
However... when I saw the anger in Takumi-kun's eyes, that cheerful feeling shattered in the blink of an eye.
"You're lying about working overtime... aren't you?@k@vS10/00/410700109. You've been out somewhere up until now, haven't you?"
"Wha...? I-I didn't go anywhere!@k@vS10/42/410600500. Where on earth would I even go?!"
"You... met up with someone, didn't you?@k@vS10/00/410700111. I don't have any money... so you went out celebrating with another man?!"
"H-huuh?!@k@vS10/42/410600502. I wouldn't do that!@k@vS10/42/410600503. Takumi-kun, what are you saying?!"
Pressured by his baseless suspicion... I unconsciously donned a rough tone.
Me? Eating out with a man besides Takumi-kun?!
Ridiculous! There must be some misunderstanding!!
Would I have come home in such a melancholy mood if that were the case?!
Just hearing Takumi-kun say that made me feel miserable.
Even though I've been working so hard!
I've been patiently enduring this, all for Takumi-kun's sake!!
How could he say that to me?!
"Huh?"
"What about you... Takumi-kun?"
...No more.
No... more.
I can't... take anymore...!
"What is that supposed to mean...?"
"...Hey, Takumi-kun?@k@vS10/42/410600507. Do you know... what my friends say about you?"
"Huuh?"
"Takumi-kun... they call you a leech."
The expression in Takumi-kun's eyes changed.
...I finally calmed down after saying that... and immediately came to regret it.
Good lord...
Did I actually say that?!
"You... Is that how you see me?!"
"No... It wasn't me... My friends..."
Tormented by guilt, I turned away to avoid looking Takumi-kun in the eyes.
...Right then, I felt a violent impact on my cheek.
It was only after I stumbled back into the wall that I realized... Takumi-kun had slapped me on the cheek.
...For the first time, ever.
Takumi-kun... hit me... for the very first time?!
"Wha...? T-Takumi-kun?!"
"Well excuse me...!@k@vS10/00/410700118. Deep down... you've been looking down on me this whole time, haven't you?!"
"I already knew those rumors!@k@vS10/00/410700120. The old hags in the neighborhood have been saying as much!!"
"I don't need someone like you telling me as much!!@k@vS10/00/410700122. Your face said it well enough!!"
Then, Takumi-kun grabbed me by the hair and tossed me to the floor.
"S... stop it!@k@vS10/42/410600512. Wh-what are you doing...?! Ghh!"
Just as I started suffocating... I came to realize this was reality.
And my field of view darkened over... thanks to the spine-chilling fear and despair.

"Even as you said you'd watch over my dreams... you've been looking down on me all this time, haven't you?@k@vS10/00/410700124. After all, I'm the scum of society!@k@vS10/00/410700125. I'm not like you!@k@vS10/00/410700126. You enjoy the sense of superiority you feel around me, don't you?!"
I... had never seen Takumi-kun like that before.
I couldn't believe it.
Takumi-kun was always so kind... so to hear him use such foul language... and to get violent with me... was incredibly shocking.
How much time passed after that?
Before I knew it, I had fallen unconscious. And when I opened my eyes... Takumi-kun was there.
"Yae... I'm sorry, I'm sorry...!!"
As I laid there motionless... Takumi-kun looked toward me with the usual shine in his eyes.
"Takumi, kun...?"
Takumi-kun seemed even more shocked than me that he resorted to violence.
"Sorry...@k@vS10/00/410700136. Yae... I'm sorry...@k@vS10/00/410700137. I... did something inexcusable...!"
Takumi-kun held me tight and looked straight into my eyes.
"I'm sorry...@k@vS10/00/410700139. I hurt you... I'm... an idiot...@k@vS10/00/410700140. I'm sorry. I'm sorry..."
"...Takumi-kun."
Takumi-kun pressed an ice pack against my swollen red cheek, with tears in his eyes.
"I'm sorry... I'm sorry..."
"I...@k@vS10/42/410600527. said something terrible... I'm sorry..."
Takumi-kun was crying as he hugged me.
And I cried too, clinging to Takumi-kun. And then, as I was held in his arms... I had a thought.
Was this really the person I fell in love with...?
"Takumi-kun..."
But... I was scared to admit that Takumi-kun had changed.
I just wanted to forget everything, and I kept crying.
That day... was the beginning of hell.
"From then on, Takumi-kun resorted to violence at every opportunity."

"Any time he was in a bad mood. When I held my chopsticks improperly, when I said "I'm home" too quietly, when I folded the laundry slightly differently...@k@vS10/42/410600531. All of those would result in a beating."
"I... was even hospitalized for it."

"That was a day when I was suddenly asked to work overtime.@k@vS10/42/410600534. It was a rare occasion when Takumi-kun was making dinner...@k@vS10/42/410600535. But in the end, I couldn't get home until 11 PM."
"He slapped me as soon as I came home, and after that..."
It was hard to imagine someone as calm and earnest as Towada-san living a life like that.
Towada-san smiled a bit as she told her story, but it looked painful.

"...After once or twice more, I gradually started to wonder if he'd ever stop.@k@vS10/42/410600538. I didn't know what I could do on my own.@k@vS10/42/410600539. And I couldn't afford to think about which might be worse."
"Plus, that was a bit of a tumultuous time..."
Rumors were going around that Yae Towada was going through a rough patch in her personal life.
And rumors spread in the neighborhood that she was supporting a man leading a hopeless lifestyle, which made it harder to do things around the neighborhood.
"My mind and body... were literally at their limits.@k@vS10/42/410600542. What even brought me joy...? It was hard to tell anymore."
"...In that position, I would've run away.@k@vS10/41/410200781. I... would have broken up with a person like that."
Towada-san forced a weak smile.
Despite that, I think I still loved him... It felt like that was what her smile was trying to convey.
"Takumi-kun would always hit me until he was satisfied, then apologize while crying his eyes out...@k@vS10/42/410600544. And I always forgave him..."
"Because deep in my heart, the memories of the old Takumi-kun were still there..."
Takumi-kun's eyes didn't sparkle like before, but his gentle smile was just the same.
He no longer focused on his dreams, and only ever drank, but those gentle times were still there.
...He'd greet me in a warm voice, "Welcome back."
He'd say, "You must be tired," and gently rub my shoulders...
No matter how bad the dishes I cooked turned out, no matter how poorly I seasoned them, he'd eat the whole thing like it was completely delicious.
That was... the Takumi-kun I loved...
"It was painful, even for me.@k@vS10/42/410600547. ...I thought about leaving Takumi-kun... many times."
"I couldn't walk out on Takumi-kun when he was unable to pay the rent on his own... but I should've been able to have my own life..."
"The Takumi-kun in front of me right now is different from the Takumi-kun I loved... I told myself that many times.@k@vS10/42/410600550. But... I couldn't do it."
"No matter what, I still saw the old Takumi-kun.@k@vS10/42/410600552. Whenever the two of us were together, I could only remember the good old times..."
"So, you couldn't abandon him.@k@vS10/62/410101522. And since you no longer knew what to do, you killed him?"
"That's not it.@k@vS10/42/410600554. ...That's... not it..."
Towada-san wracked her brain to squeeze the words out... while tightly clenching her chest.
A line of tears... fell from her eyes.
"I'm sure...@k@vS10/42/410600556. it was even more painful for Takumi-kun than it was for me..."
Then, she spit that out... the words practically bursting from her chest.
"Giving up on your dreams, falling out with your friends. I was the only person he had left.@k@vS10/42/410600558. But... even I was trying to cast Takumi-kun aside."
"I... should have left Takumi-kun immediately.@k@vS10/42/410600560. I wish I had packed all my things and left that place."
"But I couldn't do it...@k@vS10/42/410600562. Even though I was clinging to my old memories, and he kept beating me and beating me, I stayed by his side."
"...And at the same time, spending all this time around Takumi-kun made me hate how much he had changed.@k@vS10/42/410600564. ...Takumi-kun must have felt the conflict in my heart."
Towada-san said, "That's why..." then took a deep breath before continuing.
"That's why... I wanted to end everything..."
"Those lovely memories, the painful ones from recent times trying to drown them out, and even Takumi-kun himself all had to disappear."
"And so... I "got rid" of Takumi-kun.@k@vS10/42/410600568. After his death, only those happy memories would be left behind, and I would be able to rest easy... That's what I believe..."
"Like hell a selfish excuse like that will fly!@k@vS10/62/410101525. In the end, you're just making excuses to justify what you've done!!"
Without thinking, I got fired up and shouted that.
As far as I was concerned... Towada-san's explanation was nothing more than self-satisfaction.
No matter what excuses she made, no matter how beautifully she portrayed it... murder is murder!
Nothing more, nothing less!
...Still, she didn't look the least bit scared like she was up until a moment ago... She spoke quietly with a cold expression, like the spirit possessing her had finally let go.
"...Yes, that's true.@k@vS10/42/410600570. But I decided this on my own. This was my choice.@k@vS10/42/410600571. I decided this so that both of us could be happy..."
"So, I set out to accomplish the goal I planned out.@k@vS10/42/410600573. To get rid of all the unpleasant things.@k@vS10/42/410600574. ...And I put my plan into motion."
Right around that time, the Weekly Seven released the occult special edition.
She just happened to read it... and that was the turning point... where she finally formulated her plan.

"It's a really nice place. It's surrounded by nature and it won't be filled with tourists.@k@vS10/42/410600576. If we go to a place like that, maybe your mood will turn around.@k@vS10/42/410600577. Let's go together. What do you say?"
"...Takumi-kun gladly took me up on that offer."
"After that, I let him drink alcohol laced with sleeping pills, then strangled him to death while he was helplessly intoxicated, and it was all over."
"But I did make one miscalculation... Since this was an abandoned village, I assumed nobody would notice the lights here."
As Towada-san told us her story, it seemed like deep down she made peace with her past.
When she finished her long tale of the past, she looked relieved.

"I know that murder is wrong.@k@vS10/42/410600583. ...I didn't want to do it, and I don't think I'll ever do it again.@k@vS10/42/410600584. But... this was the best option.@k@vS10/42/410600585. There was... no other way."
"Y... you're insane..."
"Because... it feels so refreshing now, you know?@k@vS10/42/410600587. That alone is proof I didn't make the wrong choice."

"If you push yourself too hard and keep your feelings to yourself, in the end the unhappiness will only pile up.@k@vS10/42/410600589. Plus, Takumi-kun was released from his suffering and I was able to start my life over.@k@vS10/42/410600590. From now on, I'll be able to live my life facing a brand new tomorrow."
"It's a happy ending for both of us.@k@vS10/42/410600592. ...Don't you think?"
"...Like hell, dumbass."
I quickly took offense to Towada-san's defiant attitude.
The woman standing before me with a docile expression was, in truth, a murderer wearing the skin of a human.
Regardless of her past, regardless of the circumstances, murder isn't acceptable.
"...So... how much of that story is actually true?"
Unspeakable disgust was swirling around inside my heart.
No matter the context, no matter the situation... there is no righteous reason for depriving another human being of their life...
She kept talking about what she did without a hint of remorse or discomfort.
"In general... it's best not to trust anything a murderer says.@k@vS10/62/410101530. You say he was violent, but how much of that was the truth?"
"In reality, you had a far more ugly motive, right...?@k@vS10/62/410101532. A dead man can't tell his side of the story, after all."
"Dressing the situation up and lying to yourself is far easier than facing your sins."
"I'm not lying to myself."
Towada-san looked back at me with an expression that said she was completely done.
It made me feel sick.
Looking so proud, even after committing murder...!
Wasn't she just too weak to break up with Kurosawa...?!
Regardless of her reasons, they don't justify murder.
Shouldn't Kurosawa have had the right to live...?!
"You don't know the truth.@k@vS10/42/410600595. Takumi-kun is already dead.@k@vS10/42/410600596. ...So there's no way to learn anything more, right...?"
"Besides... these days, Takumi-kun hardly even spoke to me...@k@vS10/42/410600598. Even if you asked, I'm sure he wouldn't have said anything."
"...You can't be sure of that, can you?"
"That's right, I can't... Not anymore.@k@vS10/42/410600600. But if you were in the same position, would you have done anything differently?"
"...I did the best I could.@k@vS10/42/410600602. I don't regret my choices."
"How can you feel no shame...?"
"...Towada-san."
The next moment.
Before I could say anything more, Miyuki-chan opened her mouth.
"I want to confirm this once more.@k@vS10/40/410300883. ...That's how you truly feel, is it?"
"Yes."
Towada-san spoke resolutely. Her eyes were as clear as a blue summer sky.
Miyuki-chan, on the other hand, had a chilling glimmer in her eyes, like an intense snowstorm.
"Then that means...@k@vS10/40/410300885. ...You denied the memories and existence of the happy times you spent with the man you loved."
"No... that's not all.@k@vS10/40/410300887. You even denied... the circumstances that caused you to meet him.@k@vS10/40/410300888. As if everything was a mistake, right from the start..."
Miyuki-chan's scathing words caused Towada-san's ample smile to stiffen in an instant.
...In that moment, Miyuki-chan looked angry.
Her face was bursting with intense fury, from the bottom of her heart.

"I've been thinking about the story you just told us, but...@k@vS10/40/410300890. everything from start to finish was based in your own claims, wasn't it?@k@vS10/40/410300891. That, or your assumptions."
"...You never tried listening to him at all. You have no idea how he felt."

"You doubted his feelings based on your own arbitrary values, and after thinking it through... simply chose the path you alone thought was best.@k@vS10/40/410300894. And then you forced it upon him..."
"...What are you saying?@k@vS10/42/410600606. I didn't force anything upon him.@k@vS10/42/410600607. If anything, he's the one who forced me to endure..."

"...Did he ask for that?@k@vS10/40/410300896. Did he tell you to put up with it?@k@vS10/40/410300897. Let's live together,@k@vS10/40/410300898. pay the bills,@k@vS10/40/410300899. take care of me,@k@vS10/40/410300900. keep quiet while I abuse you..."
"...Shut up."
"...In the end, you're the one who wanted all that and let him do it, weren't you?"
"Because you were worried that if you ever complained to him or told him how you really felt, he might realize he was no longer needed and walk out on you."
"Even if that would never happen, you were afraid to even consider the possibility."
"...You still don't understand?@k@vS10/40/410300905. No matter how much it hurt, you should've been the person who best understood him."
"And you got drunk on your lofty notion of being patient with him, just so you could fulfill your own desire to keep him bound to you."
That's right... no matter how she worded it, she was just trying to justify her actions.
What Towada-san told us was... to put it bluntly, the epitome of self-satisfaction.
Thinking by yourself, making your own decisions, and constantly running away from confronting the problem in its truest sense, all in a world of your own invention... That was the logic of cowards.

"...But you slowly grew weary of it.@k@vS10/40/410300908. ...Despite that, you couldn't stand the notion of him being with anyone other than yourself.@k@vS10/40/410300909. Which resulted in... this crime."
"The future you wanted... was keeping him all to yourself, like a pet... Hiding him away in a place where nobody else could see."
"With no regard for his feelings. Even keeping your memories of him after death to yourself..."
"Shut uuuuuup!!!"
Towada-san roared... like she was coughing up blood.
Otobe and I immediately froze in place... Miyuki-chan was standing directly in front of Towada-san, bearing the brunt of Towada-san's outburst and hateful glare.
"Wrong? Are you saying it's wrong to think that way?!"
"Yeah, that's right!@k@vS10/42/410600612. Even from the start, he and I had different values!"

"I just wanted everyday happiness, but Takumi-kun wanted to gamble everything hunting for glory.@k@vS10/42/410600614. I could only take the easy path, while Takumi-kun could risk everything for a momentary chance!"
"Ahahahahaha, we were a terrible match!@k@vS10/42/410600616. We were an absurd contradiction, even from the start!!"
Towada-san's composed smile... vanished as she continued shouting.
This was... the true voice inside her heart.
"But... but I loved him!@k@vS10/42/410600618. I truly adored him, from the bottom of my heart!!"

"But I wanted to live a normal, everyday life!!@k@vS10/42/410600620. Having children, raising a family!!@k@vS10/42/410600621. We didn't even need a lawn, just a place where the whole family could relax together!!"
"Nothing luxurious, but maybe going out shopping or something on Sundays!!@k@vS10/42/410600623. Being proud of our friends' children... but always thinking our kids are the best!!"

"...Growing old together... basking in the sunlight, side by side... while watching our children grow up...!!@k@vS10/42/410600625. Looking back on the good old days nostalgically... but telling each other... that our happiest time together is always right now...!!"
It sounded completely ordinary.
Nothing glamorous about it... just a simple life.
But in reality... that alone was more than enough.
Always spending your days together with the one you love, never asking for anything more...!
"Towada-san..."
"But, still...!!@k@vS10/42/410600627. To do that, Takumi-kun would have to abandon his way of life, wouldn't he?!@k@vS10/42/410600628. He'd have to abandon his dreams, right?!"
"Could you suggest something so cruel?!@k@vS10/42/410600630. I couldn't say it! There was no way I could say it!!@k@vS10/42/410600631. I was terrified!!"
"I was scared... Scared of telling that to someone like him who lived for his dreams... of making him realize that we walked different paths... of having him leave meeee!!"
Then, Towada-san collapsed on the spot... crying her eyes out, with her face down on the tatami floor.
Through caring for him and understanding him... she realized they held different values.
Then, she was finally able to see it... the collapse of their future.
But even so, she loved him too much to accept that and let him go... which is why she chose the path to hell.
In the face of her unthinkable sorrow and despair... there was nothing left for any of us to say.
Just then,
"...Hey, Towada.@k@vS10/43/410400578. If things really are as you say, why not ask the deceased directly?"
"Have you forgotten?@k@vS10/43/410400580. This is the village where the dead dwell.@k@vS10/43/410400581. It's a simple matter to speak to the dead here."
Sonozaki-san stepped in front of us while we were all still in shock, knelt down next to Kurosawa, and put a hand on his forehead.
"Was it truly... just as you thought?@k@vS10/43/410400583. Let's hear it directly from the man in question."
We all watched Sonozaki-san, half-doubting... no, completely doubting what she said.
Is something like that... truly possible?
It couldn't be a joke... Sonozaki-san wouldn't say something like that at a time like this.
In which case, perhaps...?
The next moment... we were too shocked for words.
I had no idea what was happening. It was beyond understanding.
But even though Kurosawa was dead... I'm certain I could hear his voice...
"Ta... Takumi-kun?!"
This was the first time I'd heard Kurosawa's voice.
But it was familiar to Towada-san... She could tell after hearing only a single word.
"Yae... I'm sorry...@k@vS10/00/410700144. You were suffering... thanks to me...@k@vS10/00/410700145. Really... I'm sorry..."
"Wh... what...?@k@vS10/42/410600637. Wh-what do you mean...?!"
Looking closer, Kurosawa's lips weren't moving.
I didn't know where the voice was coming from... but it sounded like the voice was coming from directly inside my head.
"...You probably won't believe me at this point.@k@vS10/00/410700147. But... honestly, I hated myself..."

"Every time I took out my anger on you, I felt like an ugly coward. The worst kind of person.@k@vS10/00/410700149. I'd ask myself how I allowed this to happen... and I'd feel miserable and regret it afterwards..."
"Wh... what is this...?"
Towada-san stared at Kurosawa, dumbfounded.
I couldn't believe it... but I couldn't shut my ears... not at a time like this.
"I resorted to violence against you any time I lost my cool.@k@vS10/00/410700151. I hated myself for it... and I wanted to completely change myself."

"I vowed that I'd do my utmost to stay by your side, so many times.@k@vS10/00/410700153. ...But despite constantly telling myself that, I couldn't help but break that vow any time something unpleasant came to pass. How utterly pathetic..."
"It... wasn't Yae I wanted to beat to death.@k@vS10/00/410700155. ...It was myself."
"Takumi-kun... what are you saying...?"
Towada-san passionately gazed at Kurosawa's corpse.
Though Kurosawa's corpse didn't stir, I could clearly hear his voice.
"I... was afraid you'd give up on me.@k@vS10/00/410700157. I realized... that you were just seeking everyday happiness."

"But I wasn't sure if I could turn over a new leaf and live that lifestyle after so long, so I got scared, hesitated, and couldn't make up my mind...@k@vS10/00/410700159. And because of that... I was seriously terrified... that you could abandon me at any moment and it wouldn't be a surprise."
"So... when you invited me to drive out here, I was happy enough to die.@k@vS10/00/410700161. ...You still loved me.@k@vS10/00/410700162. I was so happy... to learn you hadn't given up on me."
"It can't be true...@k@vS10/42/410600641. What is this...?@k@vS10/42/410600642. If this is some kind of cruel joke... Stop it!@k@vS10/42/410600643. Stop...!!"
Towada-san incoherently muttered to herself while staring at Kurosawa's corpse.
Like she couldn't even see the rest of us.
"There was... something I wanted to tell you on this trip...@k@vS10/00/410700164. I was ready to give up on my dreams."

"Didn't I tell you back then?@k@vS10/00/410700166. When you asked me if I could ever choose between my dreams or you, I chose you.@k@vS10/00/410700167. I promised that... and I wanted to deliver on that promise... So, I..."
"T-Takumi-kun...?"
"I dunno what sort of horrible disaster you're talking about, but...@k@vS10/00/410700169. If my only options are you or my music... I'd definitely choose you, Yae."
"...Really?"
"Of course.@k@vS10/00/410700171. I could live without my music, but honestly, I don't even want to imagine life without you."
I remembered... clearly.
But... even though I heard him say it, I still only half-trusted his words.
Because something like that... seemed absolutely impossible.
He loved his music more than anything else. He cherished it since before I even met him, so there was no way he would ever choose to throw it away.
So... I arbitrarily gave up.
I couldn't believe... that he loved me enough to give that up.
I simply couldn't believe... that he truly meant those words from so long ago that I'd wanted to hear the most...!

"I was thinking of getting a proper job at a company instead of part-time work...@k@vS10/00/410700173. I was ready to completely quit my music and give up on my dream of going pro, so I went out job hunting."
"But it wasn't working out...@k@vS10/00/410700175. I never got a real job after leaving college, so it's no surprise, really..."
"Every day, interviewers would turn their noses up at me. Saying terrible things as they shot me down...@k@vS10/00/410700177. It was painful... and I took it out on you... Sorry."
How painful, how humiliating... For someone praised so much by others, it must've torn him to pieces...
I understand. ...I understand because I know him that well.
But.
He was actively trying to choose me... despite the shame it put him through...?!
He chose this gloomy, worthless, contemptible woman who didn't even believe in him to the bitter end...?!
"That's a lie...!@k@vS10/42/410600648. Lies, lies, lies!!"
"I don't believe this!! I already decided not to believe in you anymore!!"
I want to believe... but I don't!!
Not after all the pain he put me through!
Not after I struggled, worried, worried, and worried more... until I finally resolved myself to put an end to it all!!
So there's no way... I'll believe this was the result!!
As I kept shouting inside my heart, I forcibly dug through my memories of betrayal, doing what I could to support a heart on the verge of collapse.
But even so.
All of that was brushed aside as a vision of his smile floated inside my head. And then...

"I'm sorry... for all the pain I've caused you...@k@vS10/00/410700181. I'd like to say I don't want you to regret the time we spent together...@k@vS10/00/410700182. But I don't have that right. I'm sorry."

"...But let me just say this.@k@vS10/00/410700184. ...I'm glad I met you.@k@vS10/00/410700185. Because when I was with you, I was able to do my best.@k@vS10/00/410700186. ...I was able to keep chasing my dreams."
"And still... I'm sorry for turning someone as gentle as you into a murderer.@k@vS10/00/410700188. That's why... I genuinely want you to live a happy life, from now on."
"Get together with a proper guy... someone not like me... and earn the happiness you truly wanted..."
"What... are you saying?!@k@vS10/42/410600652. What do you mean? What is this?!@k@vS10/42/410600653. Dead people can't talk in the first place!!@k@vS10/42/410600654. That's enough!!"
Towada-san was upset. Her eyes wandered and her breathing was erratic.
But just then... Sonozaki-san reached for Kurosawa's corpse.
She pulled a single envelope out of his back pocket... and handed it to Towada-san.
"Towada..."
It was... a letter of acceptance from a business.
"Takumi-kun...@k@vS10/42/410600657. You... really were planning to get a job...?"
"Yae... I'm sorry...@k@vS10/00/410700191. I wanted to show this to you, before all this.@k@vS10/00/410700192. And I wanted you to be happy..."
"No way...!"
"...I always loved you.@k@vS10/00/410700194. Your kindness made me so happy. And your smile was so cute.@k@vS10/00/410700195. I wanted... to see you smile, even more."

"If we could've had a kid... you would've smiled even happier... I wanted to see that...@k@vS10/00/410700197. There were... so many promises I couldn't fulfill. Really, I'm so sorry.@k@vS10/00/410700198. ...Thank you for staying with me all this time.@k@vS10/00/410700199. Honestly... meeting you... really did...@k@vS10/00/410700200. ...Bring me so much joy..."
"Takumi-kun...!"
Towada-san fell down on her face, clinging to Kurosawa and crying.
The sorrowful cry of her voice sounded like her heart had been torn apart.
"Why... Takumi-kun...@k@vS10/42/410600661. Takumi-kun... why did you have to die?!"
"Please, wake up again! Wake up!!@k@vS10/42/410600663. So I can apologize...! I need to apologize!!@k@vS10/42/410600664. I was wrong! Please, let me tell you I'm sorryyyy!!"
"You killed him.@k@vS10/43/410400586. ...Isn't that the truth?"

"...!!@k@vS10/42/410600666. Takumi-kun... I'm sorry...@k@vS10/42/410600667. Why did this...@k@vS10/42/410600668. Why didn't I ever talk to you about it...?!@k@vS10/42/410600669. I'm sorry...! I'm sorry...!!"
Overflowing... with tears of regret.
...It was too late.
It was too late for everything.
If I had acted sooner... if I had the courage to tell him how I really felt, this future could've been avoided.
My own assumptions... cost me everything.
I was wrong about everything...!!
...And then, for a while longer,
All the rest of us could do was watch silently as she nursed her painful regrets...
4 AM.
Towada-san was still sobbing while holding Kurosawa's hand.
She was still suffering from the knowledge of her sin... born from the resentment, hate, and love she felt for Kurosawa all this time.
"By the way... we still haven't heard your story, have we?"
Sonozaki-san turned toward Miyuki-chan's knapsack.
There was certainly more than just camping equipment in there... like the handgun she used to fight the men in black.
"Yes... that's true.@k@vS10/40/410300915. At this point... I suppose there's no point hiding it."

Miyuki-chan responded to Sonozaki-san's sharp gaze by shrugging her shoulders, then pulled something resembling a small black notebook from her breast pocket, flipped the cover over and turned it toward us.
There was a gold badge inside. ...A police badge.
"I thought so...@k@vS10/43/410400589. You seemed too refined to be a yakuza."
"Thank you for your flattery.@k@vS10/40/410300917. ...Ryuunosuke-san, I'm really sorry for hiding this from you all this time.@k@vS10/40/410300918. I'm... not a writer."
"I had a feeling..."

"I'm a member of the Metropolitan Police Department's anti-yakuza... I mean, organized crime division.@k@vS10/40/410300920. I've been investigating the assassination of the Sonozaki Group's leader that occurred in Okinomiya a few weeks ago..."

"I didn't want word to get out that a police officer was coming here, so I forged an identity with the help of an acquaintance who just happened to be a publisher...@k@vS10/40/410300922. I'm sorry for lying to you."
...Miyuki-chan went on to explain that she hadn't actually received approval from her superiors to conduct an investigation in Hinamizawa.
As a result, all her activities up until this point where technically done in her time off. Which was another reason she had to conceal her identity when coming to Hinamizawa.
But now that the situation necessitated the use of force, it was no longer possible to keep quiet about it...
"So you're telling me... your job is fighting organized crime?"
"That's right."
I was taken aback after hearing such a peculiar expression.
If that's the case, it explains all her strange behavior up until now.
Her adept gun handling skills, her occasional eagle-eyed glares, her speedy analysis of the corpse... those were all skills of her profession.
"...So, I was actually the object of your pursuit this whole time?"

"Yes.@k@vS10/40/410300925. ...Though I never dreamed I'd meet you in a place like this, Sonozaki-san.@k@vS10/40/410300926. Besides... even after coming here, I still don't understand the true identity of Mion Sonozaki..."
Sonozaki-san made no effort to respond to that inquiry and stifled a laugh.
Honestly... I felt relieved to learn Miyuki-chan's real identity.
First and foremost, it meant she wasn't a ghost of Hinamizawa, or with the yakuza... or a shady murderer.
"But Miyuki-chan,@k@vS10/62/410101546. why would you come to an abandoned village...?"

"...I had my sights on the sniper who attempted to assassinate the group leader Akane Sonozaki near a hospital in Tokyo.@k@vS10/40/410300928. But it didn't appear that arresting them alone would be enough to settle the incident."

"Just as I was about to investigate the background of the incident, I received intel indicating that other group members were headed to Hinamizawa.@k@vS10/40/410300930. So, I thought there might be something going on at the Sonozaki estate... and came to investigate."
"I see...@k@vS10/43/410400592. So you've dug up that much, have you?"
Sonozaki-san let out a brief sigh.
But it seems Miyuki-chan hadn't been totally lying when she came to me saying she wanted to investigate Hinamizawa.
In fact, tomorrow... or I guess technically today, she was originally planning to conduct a full-fledged investigation with another team member who was coming here on an annual holiday.
But she gained intel on the Sonozaki Group the day before her scheduled departure, so she adjusted her schedule and came here with me, thinking she might be lucky enough to find some evidence...
...But if that's the case, why lie and say she's a reporter?
When I asked her that, Miyuki-chan shrugged her shoulders. Then, she stuck out her tongue and said, "You would've been upset if I told you I was with the police, right Ryuunosuke-san?"
Well, that's true... If I knew that the person I was traveling with was a detective instead of a reporter... I probably would've declined the offer, no matter how beautiful she was.
And what's more, it'd mean there were yakuza members waiting for me, instead of ghosts...
If she had told me, this whole thing would've ended right at the start! It doesn't matter how ridiculously cute your face is, Miyuki-chan!!
It sounds bad, but she was only planning to do information gathering at first... There's no way she could've expected two sides to clash on the battlefield.
But whether it was bold or reckless... considering her position, there was nothing I could say.
"Please, wait.@k@vS10/41/410200786. You're telling me... Mion-san is a yakuza boss?!"
"Not quite... but well, something like that."
"Wha...?!@k@vS10/41/410200788. B-but why is someone like a yakuza boss in this abandoned village...?"
"Sorimachi probably already knows this, but long ago our group held considerable power in this area.@k@vS10/43/410400595. It would take forever to explain it properly, so let me summarize..."
And so, Sonozaki-san began her story.
"When the former head of the Sonozaki family died, I was supposed to be the new leader.@k@vS10/43/410400597. But they decided I was still too young, so the role went to my mother rather than me."
"...There were people out there who didn't like that development.@k@vS10/43/410400599. A section of old heavyweights within the group split off into their own splinter faction."
"Does that mean... those men in black were targeting you, Mion-san...?"
"Precisely.@k@vS10/43/410400601. Those men are trying to prevent me from being named the successor in accordance with our ancient traditions."
"So, this is an internal conflict within the Sonozaki family...?"
I thought back on the men in black who were here earlier... and shivered. In that sense, they're even worse than ghosts.
"So, Mion-san, does that mean you can become the successor as long as you follow the traditions?"
"Come on, it's not that simple."
"Not really."

"There exists a family heirloom passed down between leaders of the Sonozaki family. As long as I can obtain it, I'll be recognized as the legitimate successor according to our ancient traditions.@k@vS10/43/410400604. Because only the true successor knows its location."
"Family heirloom...?!@k@vS10/62/410101550. Is that what you were looking for in the ritual storehouse, Sonozaki-san...?!"
"Indeed. A small bell.@k@vS10/43/410400606. ...For generations, the Sonozaki family head has settled arguments with a shake of that bell."
"And with the passing of the previous head, Oryou Sonozaki, only I know the whereabouts of the bell.@k@vS10/43/410400608. ...Although even I don't know the precise location."
"I just know that "a scroll found in the ritual storehouse of the Furude Shrine" tells the precise location."
I looked at the scroll casually rolled out on the floor.
So, that's why she was reading that thing so intently...?!
"And there's one more thing.@k@vS10/43/410400611. There's an heirloom of the Three Families, the "Tamahajiki sword," which is apparently the key to reaching the bell."
As she said that, Sonozaki-san held up her sword.

"So, you understand?@k@vS10/43/410400613. Those men in black are from the splinter faction.@k@vS10/43/410400614. They don't want me to find that bell, and they intend to settle things once and for all by killing me here in this abandoned village."
"If that's the case... are those people responsible for our cars disappearing, too...?"
"Probably."
"They probably attacked Towada because they thought she was an ally of mine.@k@vS10/43/410400617. There's a good chance they intend to kill everyone here."
"Everyone...?!"
"If they see your face and think you're doing something wrong, they'll kill you.@k@vS10/43/410400619. ...It's a common occurrence in this world of ours."
A common occurrence...?
Those words drove home how she'd been living in a completely different world.
It's easy enough to say, but... what can we do?!
...That said, we're out of options.
I'm here together with the yakuza and the police. All I can do is rely on them.
That being the case... I'm better off sticking with the police!
"According to the scroll, the bell is hidden in the Sonozaki family's main house.@k@vS10/43/410400621. So, at this point I'm going to the main house."
"I'll go with you."
"Huuh?@k@vS10/62/410101555. You too, Miyuki-chan?!"

"Our enemies are likely to appear at the main house, intent on stopping Mion-san...@k@vS10/40/410300933. I had originally planned to request reinforcements from the Okinomiya police department, but it looks like that's not an option."
I could see fierce resolve on Miyuki-chan's face.
...It seems like she's willing to risk her life to secure evidence of these guys' wrongdoings.
Of course, there's still the option of waiting for the investigation team that was supposed to accompany Miyuki-chan.
...But considering that battle earlier, there was a good chance that these guys had suspected the police's involvement, meaning they'd hide everything and pull out.
And if that happened, the police would be letting a big fish right out of their net. ...So, she had no time to waste.
Then, Sonozaki-san had a huge grin on her face, like she had figured out Miyuki-chan's intentions.
"...My, you're an unruly girl."
"Thank you."
It felt like I could feel Sonozaki-san's unyielding spirit through her smiling face.
Perhaps Miyuki-chan's reckless behavior reminded Sonozaki-san of her past self.
"How about you, Arakawa-san...?"
"Are you... actually serious...?"
If I went along with Miyuki-chan... what could I do...?!
Since we were all in the same boat, did that mean we had no other choice but to throw ourselves into the middle of a yakuza conflict?!
"Ryuunosuke-san, what would you like to do?@k@vS10/40/410300938. I think it would be safer to stay here, but..."

"No, they know we're here.@k@vS10/43/410400624. It's only a matter of time until they come back here, with even more men.@k@vS10/43/410400625. And they won't go easy on you just because you're civilians."
So in short, they'll kill us if we stay here.
"But they'll never expect us to attack their stronghold directly.@k@vS10/43/410400627. We can use that to our advantage."
"In other words, I think it would be best for all of you to come with us."

"...Having said that, you will face all manner of dangers, so I'm not sure how well I can protect all of you.@k@vS10/43/410400630. I'll do my best, but given the situation I can't guarantee you'll survive."
Otobe had been thinking it over for a while, but before long he raised his head with a resolute look on his face.
"I'll... go with you.@k@vS10/41/410200795. No, please take me with you!"
"O-Otobe?!"
"I've already thrown my life away once before.@k@vS10/41/410200797. And if I go with, I might be able to do something to help you two.@k@vS10/41/410200798. ...Would that be all right?"
Hold on, when did he start sounding so dependable...?!
"What about you, Arakawa?"
"O... of course, I'll go too!@k@vS10/62/410101560. Even if the odds are against us, I'll do everything in my power to protect Miyuki-chan!"
Really though, given our recent history, I'll be the one needing protection...
Miyuki-chan carefully looked me over, with an expression that seemed to say, "Are you really ok with that?"
Well, she's probably feeling guilty about getting me involved, but... no matter what, I'm a man. I'm prepared for what's coming.
"The rain... seems to have stopped."
It was starting to get a little brighter outside the window.
The sun was beginning to rise and the birds had started to sing.
Sonozaki-san tightly tied her hair behind her head.
"Let's go."
With that one statement, we rose to our feet at once.
However... Towada-san didn't move. She continued absentmindedly sitting next to Kurosawa's corpse.
"Towada-san...?"
"I'll stay here..."
"Wh-what are you saying...?!"
"Towada-san! Didn't you hear what we were just talking about?!@k@vS10/41/410200800. The yakuza will kill you if you stay here!"
Towada-san shook her head to cut him off, then looked up with tears in her eyes.
"It's all right.@k@vS10/42/410600672. ...I'd like to stay here a little longer... with Takumi-kun...@k@vS10/42/410600673. I want to think things through on my own..."
Towada-san shrugged her shoulders once more, probably thinking back on what Kurosawa said earlier.
Her voice was muffled, and the tears were still coming out.
"But... ah."
"Let her do as she wishes."
"Mion-san..."
"I don't think you'll be able to handle this, but maybe you'll be able to use it as a bluff."
As she said that, Sonozaki-san gently handed her a handgun.
Towada-san deeply bowed her head... with huge tears still filling her eyes.
"Thank you... very much..."
Towada-san turned her body back toward Kurosawa's corpse again. Even though his lips weren't moving, she intently gazed at him as though she could hear him speaking.
It seemed like she was trying to repent for her actions, but it also seemed like she was trying to quietly communicate once more with the love she had lost.
Either way... it wasn't our place to interfere.
So, we made our way out of the assembly hall, leaving Towada-san behind.
